cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into and Retreat from the Gold Heist
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Archie initiates a secret mission to steal Kuwaiti gold, plans its complex smuggling, then ultimately rejects full complicity after Troy's death, marking a shift from ambition to moral reckoning.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Archie proposes the gold heist and secures team cooperation through authority and secrecy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Archie refuses to leave with the gold, acknowledging the moral cost of the mission after Troy's death.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The fate of the stolen gold and whether any will be recovered or exposed remains unclear.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Leadership Under Duress and Physical Decline
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Despite injury and fever, Archie maintains command during escape and extraction planning, issuing precise orders and retaining authority even as his health deteriorates.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Archie leads the final escape prep at the bunker door, injured but in control.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Archie continues to direct operations via radio while visibly ill, asserting command from a distance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His worsening physical condition threatens his ability to survive or complete the mission.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Authority vs. Pragmatic Survival
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Archie resists dehumanization—refusing monkey meat, protecting civilians, and rejecting moral compromise—while justifying deception and violence as necessary for survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Archie refuses the live monkey, recognizing dignity in starving Iraqis.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Archie enforces Marine impersonation as necessary, despite Troy's moral resistance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Tension between ethical conduct and survival-driven actions remains unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Negotiating Power and Cooperation in Isolation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Archie attempts to unify disparate groups through shared purpose but ultimately relies on material incentives and hierarchical authority to secure cooperation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Archie rallies U.S. and Iraqi soldiers around unity but fails to gain vehicles without payment.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Archie commands Walter and Hash to execute bribes, using money rather than ideology to ensure compliance.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His reliance on bribes and coercion risks betrayal or exposure.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Medical and Spiritual Authority in Crisis
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Doc establishes and maintains dual authority as both a medical expert and spiritual figure, asserting clinical knowledge in life-or-death situations while relying on faith-based reassurance under pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: positions himself as source of medical and spiritual guidance during mission briefing
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: restrained from unnecessary medical action but confirms trust in superior's judgment under chemical threat
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: tension between clinical training and battlefield decision-making under stress

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral Evolution in Combat and Aftermath
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Doc transitions from using coercion to extract information to protecting civilians and honoring fallen comrades, reflecting a shift from self-interest to moral responsibility.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: uses threats and physical force against a cooperative Iraqi to gain intel
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: leads refugee crossing and commits to honoring Vig's final wishes
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: logistical and ethical challenges of fulfilling posthumous promises in hostile territory

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fractured and Restored Brotherhood with Vig
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Doc's relationship with Vig evolves from ideological conflict over race to reconciliation and deepened loyalty, culminating in honoring Vig's death as family.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: enters verbal conflict with Vig over racial stereotypes
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: commits to carrying out Vig's spiritual wish and treats him as family
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: emotional burden of surviving and bearing responsibility for fallen comrade

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Looting to Mission Discipline
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Doc shifts from personal gain through looting and spiritual justification to full commitment to the group mission, including rescue and survival.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: seeks team buy-in for mission using spiritual claims while holding medical supplies
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: renews commitment to mission and group cohesion despite risks
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: tension between personal desire and collective discipline under extreme conditions

cross_scene_arcs.title: Descent into Moral Crisis and Reckoning with Violence
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Troy's initial act of violence triggers psychological distress, which is then amplified by a chaotic celebration that frames killing as spectacle; later, he witnesses further atrocities and becomes increasingly unsettled by the dehumanizing nature of war.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: psychologically overwhelmed after shooting a surrendering soldier
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: morally unsettled by execution of a raped woman and growing awareness of war's brutality
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: whether his actions can be justified within the context of war

cross_scene_arcs.title: From Conspirator to Reluctant Humanitarian
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Troy transitions from a soldier seeking escape through illicit gain to a participant in a criminal mission, then ultimately engages in a humanitarian act by helping civilians access water, marking a shift toward moral reengagement.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: reluctant conspirator motivated by financial desperation
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: actively supporting survival of Shiite civilians

cross_scene_arcs.title: Fatherhood as Moral Anchor Amid War
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Troy's identity as a new father surfaces in moments of crisis, shaping his emotional responses and culminating in a shared recognition of fatherhood with an enemy interrogator, grounding his humanity amid violence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: disoriented by the tension between killing and fatherhood
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: recognizes shared grief and responsibility with another father across enemy lines
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: how to reconcile his role as a father with his participation in war

cross_scene_arcs.title: Struggle Between Obedience, Survival, and Autonomy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Troy resists mission escalation when injured, asserts autonomy by refusing an order, later makes a decisive moral choice to spare a life, and ultimately sacrifices himself in a protective act, showing evolving agency under duress.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: critically injured while attempting to save Vig
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: survival uncertain despite final acts of courage and restraint
